subject: Re: Incomplete Boxnet Retraining
body:
Hi Alister,

So I tried to retrain one of the BoxNet2Mask networks after going back 
through and using the mask tool to apply a mask to all selected gold 
fiducials.  Unfortunately, I got a similar result as my last post.  Since 
the BoxNet2Mask recognizes background, particles and high-contrast 
artifacts, do I need to select both particles and high-contrast artifacts 
to correctly train it?  i.e. is selecting and masking only artifacts a 
problem?  Also should I be using JUST the masking tool on the gold 
particles or selecting AND then masking.  If its the former, then I think I 
figured out the potential issue.

Also, even though the default BoxNet2Mask does not (usually) identify 
particles/artifacts in my micrographs, should I still run it on all the 
micrographs I have manually selected/masked objects in? Does this typically 
give better results or does it not matter?

Thank you for your time, I appreciate the help!
Kyle

On Wednesday, February 7, 2024 at 11:59:26 AM UTC-6 alist...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hey Kyle,
>
> The BoxNet model learns to classify every pixel as either particle, 
> background or high-contrast dirt to erase - you are currently telling it 
> your fiducials are particles not high contrast dirt to erase and should 
> instead paint over them with the masking tool
>
> Good luck!
>
> Alister
>  
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Feb 7, 2024, at 09:32, 'Kyle Messina' via Warp <war...@googlegroups.com> 
> wrote:
>
> ﻿Currently I'm trying to retrain Boxnet to mask fiducials in my tilt 
> series prior to subtomo averaging but something odd is going on during 
> retraining.  Every time I try to retrain the Boxnet models, the results 
> look similar to the attached images.  The accuracy metric barely moves to 
> the right and either stays static or barely decreases.  I've tried multiple 
> combinations of Boxnet models, in/excluding the default training images, 
> and using different sets of manually selected particles.  All combinations 
> result in an incomplete retraining to different extents with no hint as to 
> why.  There is also no error produced to my knowledge.
>
>
> If its relevant, I'm attempting to retrain the Boxnet models because the 
> default Boxnets do not seem to work on my current set of micrographs.  I 
> suspect its likely due to the high defocus of the dataset but I could be 
> wrong since I'm still new to Warp.  Any advice would be appreciated, thank 
> you!
>  
>
> --
